issue_title: Pagination of groups works incorrectly
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [x] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

The pagination on the Groups tab in the Keycloak tab works incorrectly. Fewer groups than available are listed. For example,. when there are eight groups, the pagination only shows two:

![pagination](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/5002921/b7d50d56-1e2e-4f3f-a908-5b9d6bf23468)

Also when clicking through the paginated list of 500 groups paginated by 50, you can visit much more than 10 paginated pages. Thus groups are shown double on every page.

### Version

22.0.1

### Expected behavior

- When there are eight groups and there is a 1-8 pagination I expect all eight groups.
- When there are 500 groups and I paginate by 50 I expect to be able to visit 10 pages, not more.

### Actual behavior

- Two groups are visible on a 1-8 pagination.
- I can visit roughly 25 pages of 50 groups when there are only 500 groups total.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Create eight top level groups
1. Give every top level group 50 subgroups
1. View pagination on main page (it will show fewer than eight groups)
1. Scroll through all groups of a subgroup (it will paginate wrongly, showing double entries on every page)

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
fix pagination (#22287)

fixes: #21949

issue_title: Configuration of flow execution is wiped after using admin UI drag and drop
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

Context:
Custom top level flow is created with two executions - one authenticator execution and one subflow execution with its own executions.
Scenario:
Upon drag and dropping the authenticator execution to be under the subflow execution - it joins the list of executions under the subflow, however the authenticator's config is cleared.

### Version

21.1.0, 22.0.1, nightly

### Expected behavior

The authenticator execution retains its configuration. Especially if this is done accidentally and we want to drag and drop the execution to be back on the same hierarchical level as the subflow instead of under it.

### Actual behavior

The configuration of the execution is cleared, I'm not sure if it still exists in the DB, but it's no longer displayed in the browser and if refreshed it's still cleared - the connection is severed regardless.

### How to Reproduce?

I think what I've written so far should be enough for the problem to be reproduced, I have nothing else to add to this.

### Anything else?

_No response_

keep config if it exists (#22247)

issue_title: Admin UI v2 : client credentials tab is hidden with view-client fine grained permission
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

With the Admin UI V2, in private clients details, the Credentials tab is hidden while having a fine grained permission view-client.
Client secrets are still readable via the api with this same permission so I guess it's an UI bug.
It seems to be a problem close to [this bug but for clients instead of users](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/20783)

### Version

22.0.1

### Expected behavior

For a user with fine grained view-client permission on a private client, the admin UI should show the Credentials tab.

### Actual behavior

For a user with fine grained view-client permission on a private client, the admin UI does not show the Credentials tab.

### How to Reproduce?

- Create a private client
- Give the view-client permission on this client to a user
- This user won't be able to see the Credentials tab for this client

### Anything else?

_No response_

Allow view creds tab if view fine-grained permission (#22007)

issue_title: Cannot add multi valued user attribute when using LDAP federation
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

ldap

### Describe the bug

There seems to be an issue in adding multi valued user attributes using UserAttributeLDAPStorageMapper when using LDAP federation. This issue appears at some times and then doesn't appear at other times. This can be seen for us when trying to add an attribute for example such as "roles" in keycloak UI, the attribute separated with double hash  (<role_name>##<role2_name>) to separate roles at times it only fetches first one, and skips everything after the double hash.

Seeing the stacktrace it appears that there are two stack traces (when behaving correctly vs when it behaves incorrectly)

**Stack trace for when mutli valued roles works:**
```
org.keycloak.storage.ldap.mappers.UserAttributeLDAPStorageMapper$1.setAttribute(UserAttributeLDAPStorageMapper.java:195)
org.keycloak.models.utils.UserModelDelegate.setAttribute(UserModelDelegate.java:75)
org.keycloak.models.utils.UserModelDelegate.setAttribute(UserModelDelegate.java:75)
org.keycloak.storage.adapter.UpdateOnlyChangeUserModelDelegate.setAttribute(UpdateOnlyChangeUserModelDelegate.java:69)
org.keycloak.userprofile.DefaultUserProfile.updateInternal(DefaultUserProfile.java:121)
org.keycloak.userprofile.DefaultUserProfile.update(DefaultUserProfile.java:98)
org.keycloak.services.resources.admin.UserResource.updateUser(UserResource.java:193)
java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke0(Native Method)
java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(NativeMethodAccessorImpl.java:62)
java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.java:43)
```
**Stack trace for when mutli valued roles doesnt work:**
```
org.keycloak.storage.ldap.mappers.UserAttributeLDAPStorageMapper$1.setSingleAttribute(UserAttributeLDAPStorageMapper.java:180)
org.keycloak.models.utils.UserModelDelegate.setSingleAttribute(UserModelDelegate.java:70)
**org.keycloak.storage.ldap.mappers.FullNameLDAPStorageMapper$1.setAttribute(FullNameLDAPStorageMapper.java:153)**
org.keycloak.models.utils.UserModelDelegate.setAttribute(UserModelDelegate.java:75)
org.keycloak.models.utils.UserModelDelegate.setAttribute(UserModelDelegate.java:75)
org.keycloak.storage.adapter.UpdateOnlyChangeUserModelDelegate.setAttribute(UpdateOnlyChangeUserModelDelegate.java:69)
org.keycloak.userprofile.DefaultUserProfile.updateInternal(DefaultUserProfile.java:121)
org.keycloak.userprofile.DefaultUserProfile.update(DefaultUserProfile.java:98)
org.keycloak.services.resources.admin.UserResource.updateUser(UserResource.java:193)
java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke0(Native Method)
java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(NativeMethodAccessorImpl.java:62)
java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.java:43)
```
So as it appears in the above stack trace, when the mappers are behaving incorrectly, FullNameLDAPStorageMapper#setAttribute seems to be called somehow and also during our testing when we delete the FullNameLDAPStorageMapper configured by Keycloak the issue seems to get resolved, however recreating FullNameLDAPStorageMapper starts the incorrect behaviour all over again. 

### Version

20.0.3

### Expected behavior

multiple values for the "role" attribute should work correctly and retain the values added.

### Actual behavior

multiple values for the "role" attribute at times removes all but the first value.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Create LDAP user federation.
2. Add a user-attribute-ldap-mapper (map the "roles" user model attribute to "userRole" ldap attribute
3. Create a user with the multiple values for the "role" attribute.

```
curl -k -v https://localhost:8444/auth/admin/realms/local-ldap3/users -H "Content-Type: application/json" -H "Authorization: bearer $TOKEN" --data '{
    "username": "testuser1",
    "enabled": true,
    "attributes": {
        "uidNumber": ["800"],
        "roles": ["SystemAdmin", "AnotherRole"]
    }
}
```

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Fix multi-valued LDAP attribute support

FullName LDAP storage mapper was delegating to single-valued setter even
when multi-valued setter was called.

Closes #22091

Signed-off-by: Tero Saarni <tero.saarni@est.tech>

issue_title: Occasional operator test failures
issue_body:
### Description

Recent prs seem to be related to some failed startup / migration https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/actions/runs/5726457904/job/15517348959?pr=22152#step:8:2561 and https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/actions/runs/5726457904/job/15517348959?pr=22152#step:8:2699 - this does not happen consistently, but once it does it seems to inhibit that keycloak instance from coming up.  Presumably this has to do with sharing the database instance for all tests in a test class.

The root exception message is 
```
Caused by: org.postgresql.util.PSQLException: ERROR: column "user_setup_allowed" of relation "authentication_execution" does not exist
```

This may have become more prevelent by changes to speed up the tests - the immediate termination of pods and not waiting for instances to become ready could cause a prior test to corrupt some expection of the database state.  However I'm not familar enough to know why it should even be entering migration logic.

cc @vmuzikar

issue_title: Operator secrets sequencing
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

operator

### Describe the bug

When deploying using the operator the operator checks if the db secrets exist and if they do not then it stops further deployment.

If the secrets are later provisioned this does not get picked up by the operator so the deployment becomes stuck.

Successful deployment requires the secrets are in place prior to creating the keycloak definition. This makes an implied sequencing.

Ideally the operator will retry the creation of the deployment/statefulset or it will create the stateful regardless of the secret and allow the sts and pod operations to implement the retries.


### Version

22.0.0

### Expected behavior

Creation of the stateless set occurs regardless of the presence of the db credentials secret or the operator checks for presence of secrets after a timeout

### Actual behavior

After the initial check for secrets when the keycloak object is created and faulted no further updates or progress is made.

### How to Reproduce?

Create a keycloak definition using secret references to db username and password referring to a secret that does not exist.
The stateful set will not be created.
Create the appropriate secret.
The operator will not update.

Now delete the keycloak object then recreate.
The stateful set is now constructed. Showing that the operator has an implied sequencing that should ideally be removed or avoided to ensure consistent deployments

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Missing client_id validation match when authenticating client with JWT
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

According to JWT client authentication specification for client_id parameter https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7521.html#section-4.2 :
```
client_id
      OPTIONAL.  The client identifier as described in [Section 2.2](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7521.html#section-2.2) of
      OAuth 2.0 [[RFC6749](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc6749)].  The "client_id" is unnecessary for client
      assertion authentication because the client is identified by the
      subject of the assertion.  If present, the value of the
      "client_id" parameter MUST identify the same client as is
      identified by the client assertion.
```

Right now, Keycloak doesn't check that if client_id is present, it matches the client from client assertion. This shouldn't have any side-effect (as the most important is client from JWT), but still should be validated

### Version

22.0.1

### Expected behavior

Validation for client_id is done as per specification

### Actual behavior

When using parameter `client_id=some-nonexistent-or-nonmatching-client`, client authentication works 

### How to Reproduce?

See above

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Verification of iss at refresh token request
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

Token endpoint doesn't verify the `iss` claims of refresh token while handling a token refresh request.
(Off cause the token endpoint token refresh handler verifies the refresh token is issued from itself or not, so this behavior can be problem only when the domain of Keycloak server is changed.)
It should be noted that the issued access token and refresh token inherits the `iss` claim value from the request's refresh token with invalid `iss` claim value.
Contrary to token refresh, the token introspection endpoint verifies the `iss` claim value.
This will put the client a endless loop of token refresh when Keycloak server domain is changed:
1. After the domain of Keycloak server is changed, when the client request a token refresh with a refresh token with a old `iss` claim value, the Keycloak server issues an access token and refresh token with old `iss` claim value.
2. The client try to use the access token with old `iss` claim, but the introspection endpoint rejects the request.
3. Then, the client try to refresh the tokens with the refresh token, but the issued tokens have old `iss` claim value again.
4. Retry from the step 2 as long as the client doesn't detect the endless loop.



### Version

22.0.1

### Expected behavior

We should verify `iss` at refresh-token request. Will be good to doublecheck if we verify `iss` at other endpoints as well

### Actual behavior

It's possible we're not verifying `iss` at refresh-token request, but we're verifying at introspection-endpoint, which can cause some inconsistencies as described above

### How to Reproduce?

See above

### Anything else?

_No response_

Closes #22191

issue_title: Fix usage of deprecated API in common module
issue_body:
### Description

The common module uses some deprecated APIs as well as partially unnecessary reflection which can be avoided.

Currently reflection is used to call the protected method `liquibase.Liquibase#resetServices` . Instead of using reflection the method could be exposed as a public method by subclassing the `Liquibase` class, e.g. `KeycloakLiquibase`.
commit_message:
Fix deprecations in common module

- Use charset in `Encode` class
- Replace reflective call to protected `Liquibase#resetServices()` with call to exposed public method on a custom subclass `KeycloakLiquibase`
- Remove usage of deprecated AccessController class in Reflections
- Deprecated SetAccessibleProvilegedAction and UnsetAccessibleProvilegedAction

Fixes #22209

issue_title: URLs with custom protocols fail "Trusted Hosts" validation
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

When anonymous client-registration is enabled with verification of client URIs, The verification fails with a "URL is malformed" error (`MalformedUrlException`).

In my case, the thing trying to do client registration is a phone application with a "custom"/non-standard protocol handler.

Because `TrustedHostClientRegistrationPolicy` tries to parse the client's redirect URI as an URL, any scheme that doesn't have a matching handler in Java will cause a `MalformedUrlException`.

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/clientregistration/policy/impl/TrustedHostClientRegistrationPolicy.java#L217
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/clientregistration/policy/impl/TrustedHostClientRegistrationPolicy.java#L223

### Version

22.0.0

### Expected behavior

The validation passes and the new client is added.

### Actual behavior

The client registration is denied with an error message:

```json
{"error":"insufficient_scope","error_description":"Policy 'Trusted Hosts' rejected request to client-registration service. Details: URL is malformed"
```

### How to Reproduce?

1. Disable `Host Sending Client Registration Request Must Match` and enable `Client URIs Must Match` in the _Trusted Hosts_ client registration policy.
2. Add a `Trusted Host` entries with "custom" protocols and save the policy.
    e.g. `ios.owncloud.com`
4. Issue a POST request to `/realms/<realm-id>/clients_registration/openid-connect` with a valid payload:
    ```json
   {"application_type":"native","token_endpoint_auth_method":"client_secret_basic","redirect_uris":["oc://ios.owncloud.com"],"client_name":"ownCloud/iOS 12.0.2"}
    ```

### Anything else?

Seems like the issue would be fixed with switching to the URI class (at least for the `redirect_uri`s).
commit_message:
Switch Trusted Host policy redirect verification to URI

Switch parsing of the redirect URIs for the Trusted Host Client Registration Policy from URL to URI.
The java URL class tries to instantiate a handler for the scheme, which fails when a "custom" scheme, such as those used in phone apps is used.
In contrast, the URI class simply parses the string, ensuring the format is valid.
The other URLs (baseUrl, rootUrl, adminUrl) are still parsed as URLs.
See https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-registration-1_0.html#ClientMetadata for the Client Registration parameter documentation.

Closes #22309

issue_title: kc.sh errors if using characters like semicolon inside the arguments
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

There are errors when using the `kc.sh` script if the parameters contain problematic characters like semicolon `;`. One easy example is the mssql `db-url`:

```bash
./kc.sh start --db=mssql --hostname=localhost --db-username=keycloak "--db-url=jdbc:sqlserver://127.0.0.1:1433;DatabaseName=keycloak;integratedSecurity=false;trustServerCertificate=true;encrypt=No;sendStringParametersAsUnicode=false" "--db-password=Keycloak01!" --transaction-xa-enabled=false
Changes detected in configuration. Updating the server image.
Updating the configuration and installing your custom providers, if any. Please wait.
2023-08-09 10:47:01,142 INFO  [io.quarkus.deployment.QuarkusAugmentor] (main) Quarkus augmentation completed in 2988ms
Server configuration updated and persisted. Run the following command to review the configuration:

	kc.sh show-config

Next time you run the server, just run:

	kc.sh start --optimized --hostname=localhost --db-username=keycloak --db-url=jdbc:sqlserver://127.0.0.1:1433

./kc.sh: line 128: --db-password=Keycloak01!: command not found
```

If you see the semicolon is breaking the line and the rest of parameters are not taken into account. I think we need to quote `${VAR@Q}` the arguments in the script. I suppose there can be other errors with passwords and so on if they contain problematic chars like `$"` or similar.

As a workaround you can use the `keycloak.conf` of env variables to pass the options.

### Version

22.0.1

### Expected behavior

The `kc.sh` should work OK and the chars should be escaped/quoted.

### Actual behavior

Errors in `kc.sh`.

### How to Reproduce?

See the example execution in the description.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Mapper for client roles with multivalued disabled, still sets the value as an array
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [ ] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

In the image below we see a mapper which is intended to return the first client role assigned to the authenticating user.

When the claim type is set to JSON an unknown error is returned when calling the token endpoint.

Further, I don’t believe this is a viable solution as at best, wouldn’t we get a JSON object inserted into the token, when actually, what is required is the name of the first client role without any further adornments, i.e. no enclosing brackets.

Next, the closest I can get is to use the STRING claim type, but this wraps the name of the first client role in brackets. This has to be viewed as a bug, because the user interface clearly indicates it is not a multivalued result. When ‘Multivalued’ is toggled off, this means “get me the first value”, so singular is implicit. Providing a value typed as an array would be deemed invalid.

My target system will not accept the role wrapped in [ ], I have to find a way to get this from keycloak as just the_role not [ the_role ].

![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/43819451/15de657a-92e6-4fec-b30b-515bc29e51e6)


### Version

22.0.1

### Expected behavior

jwt return :
```
"https://hasura.io/jwt/claims": {
    "x-hasura-default-role": "tenant_admin",
    "x-hasura-user-id": "1f5d0070-eda0-4937-bf59-3123d1fc88d0",
    "x-hasura-allowed-roles": [
      "tenant_admin",
      ...
    ]
  },
```

and
When the claim type is set to JSON an unknown error is returned when calling the token endpoint. 500 CORS  ，and  webOrigin is  not working

### Actual behavior

jwt return :
```
"https://hasura.io/jwt/claims": {
    "x-hasura-default-role": "[tenant_admin]",
    "x-hasura-user-id": "1f5d0070-eda0-4937-bf59-3123d1fc88d0",
    "x-hasura-allowed-roles": [
      "tenant_admin",
      ...
    ]
  },
```

or 
fix : When the claim type is set to JSON an unknown error is returned when calling the token endpoint. 500 CORS 

### How to Reproduce?

I am integrating Keycloak with Hasura. It expects a value x-hasura-default-role to be (in my case) ‘tenant_admin’ or ‘tenant_user’, which will be assigned from a Client Role.

So as per the tooltip on the multivalued option, I specify that the value I want is not multivalued, expecting it will take the first role assigned to the user from the Client Roles. So why is the resulting value wrapped in ‘[’ ‘]’ when it is not multivalued? Refer x-hasura-default-role: “[tenant_admin]”.

This causes my authorisation to fail with Hasura.
![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/43819451/4df0f1a6-96a6-414d-b4a5-2d7ec58815c8)

When i try to switch Claim JSON Type to JSON,Doing so appears to cause a 500 error accessing the token endpoint.

Just to see if a valid token would be formed I changed the value from STRING to JSON, but I suspect even if this had worked, Hasura would not then correctly interpret this value as it expect just the name of a role as plain text.

But here is my Keycloak config showing JSON is selected, and the resultant 500 error which seems to occur when the token endpoint is called.
![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/43819451/35744a12-52f9-4227-8577-925e5b3549f2)


### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Only first kerberos provider is checked
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

authentication

### Describe the bug

When two or more Kerberos providers are added to the same realm the client provided token is only validated towards the first kerberos provider.. If the client provides a token issued by the second Kerberos provider they are not able to login.

The problem seems to be in `org.keycloak.storage.UserStorageManager#getUserByCredential` that fails completely on the first failure. It should instead continue to authenticate towards the next storage provider, if there are any.

### Version

22.0.1

### Expected behavior

Each Kerberos storage provider should be checked in the order specified by priority until one is successful or there are no more providers are available.

### Actual behavior

Only the first Kerberos provider is actually checked.

### How to Reproduce?

Setup a keycloak realm with two kerberos providers, test to login to the "Account Manager" with a token issued by the kerberos kdc defined in the provider with the least priority.

A demo using "docker compose" to setup a test environment together with a detailed guide on how to  reproduce this bug can be found here https://github.com/ullgren/keycloak-with-two-kdc

### Anything else?

Proposed patch will be submitted as a PR shortly.

Fallback to next LDAP/Kerberos provider when not able to find authenticated Kerberos principal (#22531)

closes #22352 #9422
